dimensions.note: No secrets handled or stored. Uses the ambient AWS credential chain or per-profile fromIni({ profile }); stackSetName/callAs/region/profile are non-secret globalArguments. Nothing is vaulted and no credential material is logged.

info on audit completion of the reads (stackset name + instance + operation counts) and on the final summary (verdict + rows written). Structured placeholders ({name}, {count}, {verdict}); no secrets logged. (No warn/error paths exist because the read sweep either succeeds wholesale or throws — see error-handling.)

runAudit performs the three reads (describeStackSet, listStackInstances, listOperations) before any writeResource, so any AWS failure throws out of execute before a partial summary/instance can be written — the workflow engine marks the step failed with no inconsistent partial state. This is the correct posture for a single-target audit (unlike the multi-target sg-audit, there is no per-target sweep to isolate). SDK shapes are all treated as optional and defaulted (?? '', ?? [], ?? 0) before use, so a sparse-but-valid response renders rather than throwing on a missing field.

tests/ holds a pure-logic unit suite (classifyFailure across every status/reason branch incl. the iam-name-conflict fingerprint, countBy, buildRootCauses exclusion+ranking, detectPatterns for both the IAM multi-region collision and drift-never-detected — with an explicit assertion the narrative names the sibling extension and NOT an in-code detectDrift — and the conservative deriveSafeToReapply matrix) and a smoke suite driving runAudit through an in-memory StackSetApi (1 summary + 1 instance per stack instance, keyed instance-<account>-<region>; collision -> 'no' verdict + root cause; empty stackset; in-flight op -> 'no'; recentOperations cap). The colocated aws_stackset_audit_test.ts pins name/manifest+version sync, the resource/method key sets (asserting the single read-only 'audit' method, no mutating method), and the upgrade-chain-ends-at-version invariant. Both success and failure-classification paths covered. Re-verified the full suite (34 tests) passes after the published-surface-hygiene scrub of the stackSetName describe example.

Read-only sweep with deterministic instance keys instanceKey(account, region) and a fixed 'summary' key, so re-runs overwrite the same instances rather than duplicating (smoke test asserts the exact key set). No created cloud resources to orphan. Because all reads precede all writes, an interrupted run leaves no partial data.

DescribeStackSet, ListStackInstances, and ListStackSetOperations all use the native CloudFormation API with CallAs threaded through. listStackInstances paginates to full exhaustion via NextToken (MaxResults 100). listOperations is a bounded 'most recent N' selection (default 15, capped 1..100): it stops once out.length reaches the limit and slices to it — this is a documented recent-window, not an undisclosed truncation of a complete dataset (the summary field is labelled 'recent operations'). Throttling is handled by the SDK retryer (maxAttempts 8). The abortSignal from context.signal is threaded into every command so a cancelled execution stops promptly. All SDK shapes treated as optional and defaulted before use.

A single CloudFormationClient is created per run inside the facade and used only for read calls; the AWS SDK client needs no explicit teardown. No file handles, sockets, or temp files are opened, and no cloud resources are created, so there is nothing to clean up on any code path. The AbortSignal is propagated for prompt cancellation.

Re-reviewed after scrubbing the stackSetName global-arg describe example from 'VantaOrgSetup' (a real third-party vendor product name) to the generic 'ExampleOrgBaseline'. grep -rniE 'sitoo|vanta|aikido|688375758630|m@s\.com' over the whole extension dir now returns zero hits. README examples and test fixtures use placeholder account ids (111111111111-444444444444, all RFC-style synthetic), synthetic stackset names (DemoSet, IamSet, Empty), and synthetic OU/stack ids (ou-root-abc, arn:stack/a). No real IPs, internal hostnames, or real account ids in the entrypoint, README, or fixtures. Zero real infrastructure or vendor identifiers remain on the published surface.

All resource schemas (SummarySchema, InstanceRecordSchema, and the nested ParameterSchema/OperationSchema/RootCauseSchema/PatternSchema/DriftSchema/SafeToReapplySchema) are plain z.object with NO .passthrough() (confirmed by grep) — the count maps use z.record(z.string(), z.number()) and verdict is a z.enum. Public resource shapes (InstanceRecord, Operation, RootCause, Pattern, Drift, SafeToReapply) are declared as explicit interfaces/union types rather than z.infer, keeping deno doc --lint clean. (GlobalArgs uses z.infer internally but is not an exported public type.)
Both specs (summary, instance) declare lifetime: 'infinite' and garbageCollection: 10.

Read-only audit/discovery model, not a CRUD lifecycle. The single audit method is a factory that re-derives the summary + instance rows each run; there is no AWS resource this model owns to create/update/delete, and re-running audit is itself the refresh mechanism, so a separate sync method is unnecessary. The mutating drift-detection capability is deliberately excluded and shipped as a sibling extension, composed via a swamp workflow.

audit performs only read-only Describe*/List* CloudFormation calls (verified: no DetectStackSetDrift/UpdateStackSet/CreateStackInstances/etc. command or import remains — the only DetectStackSetDrift reference is a doc comment explaining the deliberate exclusion). There is no mutating action a policy/live preflight label would gate.

Mechanical check: the summary is written under the fixed key 'summary' and instance rows under 'instance-<account>-<region>', so the two specs share no key and cannot collide; instance keys are unique per (account, region). Each spec is written by a single code path. Schema-write conformance verified: the summary object carries all 24 SummarySchema fields (with the nested drift fully populating all 7 DriftSchema fields, parameters/operations/rootCauses/detectedPatterns/safeToReapply each conforming to their sub-schemas), and each instance object carries all 12 InstanceRecordSchema fields — 1:1 coverage, no placeholder-stuffed fields.

Single-pass factory writer; runAudit only calls context.writeResource and never reads back previously stored swamp state (no update/delete/sync method exists), so the readResource-vs-dataRepository concern does not arise. Each instance's drift status is read fresh from the StackSet API response, never from prior stored state.

Initial publish. model.upgrades carries a single no-op entry whose toVersion (2026.06.13.0) equals model.version, establishing the chain so any future schema-changing upgrade chains cleanly from here; the colocated test asserts this invariant. globalArguments are unchanged, so the identity upgradeAttributes (old) => old is correct. The hygiene scrub touched only the describe() text of an existing arg, not the arg shape, so no upgrade entry is implicated.
